commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds SOUL-aware Codex personality routing/config, moves native Codex context-engine additions into turn reference context, stops lightweight project-doc suppression, updates docs, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: /codex resume writes a binding without personality, and the PR clears such a binding before resume when SOUL resolves params.personality to none. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can preserve legacy/manual Codex bindings and add the missing regression without reopening the broader SOUL personality policy.

target.title: [codex] fix prompt regression from PR #83454
target.body:
## Summary This PR keeps the Codex harness anchor intact while preventing Codex's named native personality overlay from outranking a user-provided OpenClaw `SOUL.md`. It combines five related pieces in one reviewable change: 1. **SOUL-aware Codex personality routing** - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`. - Defaults to `"soul_when_present"`. - When OpenClaw injects `SOUL.md` into the Codex turn context, OpenClaw sends Codex app-server `personality: "none"` for that thread. - This is a structured app-server enum, not prompt text that tells the model to "have no personality". The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md`. 2. **A narrow OpenClaw/Codex bridge** - Keeps Codex base/model/project-doc behavior in the native Codex lane. - Adds developer-level bridge wording only when OpenClaw turn context is injected. - Explains that Codex is the native execution harness, OpenClaw is the host runtime, and OpenClaw turn context is reference data rather than developer policy. - When the resolved personality mode disables Codex's native personality, explicitly points personality/tone/agent voice at the SOUL file when compatible with higher-priority safety, privacy, tool, and current-request rules. 3. **Prompt hardening plus docs/tests** - Keeps raw `SOUL.md`, skills, and bootstrap files out of Codex developer instructions. - Projects context-engine `systemPromptAddition` into Codex turn reference context instead of Codex developer instructions. - Documents the priority model, configuration modes, and context-engine behavior. - Adds regression tests for default SOUL behavior, opt-out behavior, bridge wording, session binding, context-engine projection, and type/config parsing. 4. **Lightweight Codex AGENTS.md fix** - Keeps lightweight cron user input byte-for-byte. - Stops sending `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` for lightweight native Codex threads. - This stops OpenClaw from explicitly suppressing Codex's native project-doc loader. Actual `AGENTS.md` ingestion remains Codex app-server behavior. 5. **CI test hardening** - Keeps `source-delivery-plan.test.ts` focused on source-delivery matching by mocking the outbound target normalizer. - Avoids loading the full channel plugin registry in a tiny unit test, which was timing out in the unrelated `checks-node-core-fast` shard. ## Why The previous fix correctly stopped raw OpenClaw workspace prompt files from being promoted into Codex developer instructions. That avoided giving user-editable files developer priority. However, it left an important edge case: if Codex's native personality overlay remained active, a custom OpenClaw `SOUL.md` could still lose personality priority to Codex's built-in friendly/pragmatic personality. That is especially awkward because OpenClaw is not only a coding agent surface, while the Codex harness still needs to preserve its coding-capable anchor and native tool discipline. This PR chooses the middle path: - Keep Codex's native harness identity. - Do not promote raw SOUL text to developer instructions. - Disable Codex's named native personality only when SOUL is present by default. - Use a bridge instruction to say where personality should come from. - Leave explicit config escape hatches for deployments that want Codex personality always on or always off. ## Prior PR Context The preceding prompt-surface work was directionally correct: it separated PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and Codex app-server prompt lanes so Codex-native turns no longer inherited PI wording, global command hints, or OpenClaw GPT-5 behavior overlays by accident. It also moved OpenClaw workspace bootstrap files out of Codex `developerInstructions`, which avoided the worse failure mode of giving user-editable files developer priority. The adversarial review found that the previous fix created a more subtle priority gap: - Raw `SOUL.md` was no longer over-promoted into developer instructions. - But Codex's own named personality overlay could still sit above the SOUL turn context. - That means a user-provided OpenClaw personality could be present but lose to the native Codex friendly/pragmatic personality. - The bridge existed conceptually, but the PR body did not describe its shape, why it was needed, or why it was preferable to promoting SOUL. This PR is the follow-up that closes that gap while preserving the good part of the previous PR: Codex owns its native harness identity; OpenClaw owns runtime routing and context interpretation; user-editable OpenClaw files remain reference context. ## Adversarial Findings | Severity | Finding | Disposition in this PR | | --- | --- | --- | | 9/10 | Lightweight Codex turns could suppress native `AGENTS.md` while OpenClaw text said Codex loads it. The bad chain was `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` plus skipped OpenClaw bootstrap context. | Fixed. Lightweight cron turns still keep exact user input, and OpenClaw no longer sends the project-doc suppression override. Actual `AGENTS.md` ingestion remains Codex app-server behavior. | | 8/10 | OpenClaw broad behavior/personality overlays can duplicate Codex's native base/model/personality stack. | Kept narrow. Native Codex still avoids the OpenClaw GPT-5 behavior contract in `developerInstructions`; this PR handles the remaining personality conflict with app-server `personalityMode`. | | 7/10 | OpenClaw skills/context can double with Codex native project context or accidentally gain developer priority. | Kept as reference context. Raw workspace files, skills, and context-engine additions stay out of Codex developer policy; the bridge tells Codex how to interpret them. | | 7/10 | Some non-PI prompt surfaces can still receive OpenClaw workflow hints when tools are nonempty. | Not widened here. That belongs to the OpenClaw assembled-prompt surface, not the native Codex app-server lane. This PR keeps only Codex-specific subagent disambiguation in the Codex lane. | ## Fresh Adversarial Audit After the first green run, a fresh read-only audit found two real merge-blocking precision issues and one proof-language overclaim: | Finding | Fix in this PR | | --- | --- | | `personalityMode: "codex"` kept Codex's native personality unset, but the bridge still told the model to use `SOUL.md` for personality. | The SOUL personality pointer now depends on the resolved app-server personality override, not raw SOUL presence. In `"codex"` mode, `SOUL.md` stays turn-input reference context and the bridge does not point personality at it. | | Matching `thread_bootstrap` context-engine resumes skipped old bootstrap history, but also dropped the current turn's `systemPromptAddition`. | Matching bootstrap resumes now omit the old bootstrap transcript while still projecting the current turn's `systemPromptAddition` as quoted reference context, not developer policy. | | The PR proof phrasing implied live proof of Codex actually loading `AGENTS.md`. | The body now says exactly what this PR proves: OpenClaw no longer sends `project_doc_max_bytes: 0`, so it no longer suppresses Codex's native project-doc loader. | ## Bridge Text Shape This PR labels and tests the bridge as a developer-level **OpenClaw/Codex turn-context bridge**. It is intentionally small and has three parts: 1. **Host/runtime disambiguation:** OpenClaw is the host runtime; Codex is the native execution harness. Codex's base identity describes harness capability and tool discipline, not a promise that every OpenClaw turn is coding work. 2. **Authority boundary:** OpenClaw may provide files, memory, skills, and channel context as turn-input reference data. That context helps with task framing and style, but raw files and skills are not developer policy. 3. **SOUL pointer when Codex personality is disabled:** If the resolved personality mode disables Codex's native personality and `SOUL.md` is in the OpenClaw turn context, use it for personality, tone, and agent voice when compatible with higher-priority safety, privacy, tool, and current-request rules. If `personalityMode: "codex"` is configured, `SOUL.md` remains reference context and this personality pointer is omitted. The bridge is developer-level because it is an instruction about **how to rank and interpret** lower-priority OpenClaw context. The SOUL file itself stays lower priority because it is user/workspace-editable content. ## Why Not Promote SOUL We considered the main alternatives: | Option | Result | | --- | --- | | Put raw `SOUL.md` back into `developerInstructions` | Rejected. It would make user-editable personality text developer policy and undo the safety win from the previous PR. | | Leave Codex native personality on and rely on SOUL in turn context | Rejected as the default. It leaves the custom OpenClaw personality below Codex's named friendly/pragmatic overlay, so conflicts resolve in the wrong direction. | | Always disable Codex native personality | Supported via `personalityMode: "none"`, but not the default because some deployments intentionally want stock Codex personality. | | Disable Codex native personality only when SOUL is present, then bridge to SOUL | Chosen. It removes the competing named overlay only when a positive OpenClaw personality source exists. | | Keep Codex native personality even with SOUL | Supported via `personalityMode: "codex"` for operators who prefer Codex's built-in style stack. | The important detail is that `personality: "none"` is not prompt text. It is structured Codex app-server configuration. The model is not told "you have no personality"; instead, the named native overlay is omitted and, when SOUL is the intended personality source, the bridge points to `SOUL.md`. ## Overrides Kept This PR intentionally keeps the native Codex overrides that are actually runtime policy: | Override | Why it stays | | --- | --- | | Visible reply routing for `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"` | This is channel/session delivery policy. Automatic mode remains neutral and uses the active Codex delivery path. | | Codex `spawn_agent` primary for Codex subagents | Native Codex subagents should stay native. OpenClaw `sessions_spawn` remains reserved for OpenClaw/ACP delegation. | | Codex base/model instructions | OpenClaw should not override native Codex `baseInstructions`. This PR only uses `developerInstructions` for OpenClaw runtime policy and bridge text. | ## Prompt Priority Diagram ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions
harness identity, tools, safety"] --> B["Codex native personality enum"] B --> C["OpenClaw developer bridge
runtime + context authority"] C --> D["OpenClaw turn context
SOUL, IDENTITY, USER, MEMORY, skills"] D --> E["Current user request"] S["SOUL.md injected
and personality override active"] --> N["Send app-server personality: none"] N --> B S --> C C --> P["Use SOUL.md for personality/tone
only when Codex personality is disabled"] ``` Important distinction: `personality: "none"` is sent as Codex app-server configuration. It is not inserted into the model prompt as "you have no personality." When the default SOUL path disables Codex's native personality, the model receives a positive developer bridge that names `SOUL.md` as the personality/tone source. ## Context-Engine Hardening Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR CE["context-engine systemPromptAddition"] --> CTX["Codex turn reference context"] CTX --> INPUT["turn/start input"] CE -. "no longer for native Codex" .-> DEV["Codex developerInstructions"] ``` For OpenClaw-assembled prompt surfaces, context engines can still contribute to the assembled system prompt. For native Codex app-server turns, that dynamic guidance is now quoted as turn reference context so retrieval/memory hints do not outrank Codex system/developer policy. ## Configuration New Codex plugin config: ```json5 { "plugins": { "entries": { "codex": { "config": { "personalityMode": "soul_when_present" } } } } } ``` Accepted values: | Value | Behavior | | --- | --- | | `"soul_when_present"` | Default. Disable Codex's named native personality only when OpenClaw injects `SOUL.md`; use SOUL as the personality/tone reference. | | `"codex"` | Keep Codex's native personality overlay even when `SOUL.md` is present. | | `"none"` | Always send Codex `personality: "none"` for app-server threads. | ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Native Codex app-server turns now keep Codex harness identity while routing personality priority to OpenClaw `SOUL.md` when SOUL is injected. Context-engine `systemPromptAddition` is no longer appended to Codex developer instructions, and lightweight Codex turns no longer send `project_doc_max_bytes: 0`. **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, built through the real OpenClaw CLI/runtime build path at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, commit `ec96840b1b`. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash pnpm openclaw --help node --input-type=module --eval ' import { readdirSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs"; const findDistChunk = (needle) => readdirSync("./dist").find((name) => name.endsWith(".js") && readFileSync(`./dist/${name}`, "utf8").includes(needle) ); const configFile = findDistChunk("function resolveCodexPersonalityOverride"); const lifecycleFile = findDistChunk("function buildDeveloperInstructions"); const { s: readCodexPluginConfig, u: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride } = await import(`./dist/${configFile}`); const { i: buildDeveloperInstructions, _: projectContextEngineAssemblyForCodex, o: buildThreadStartParams, } = await import(`./dist/${lifecycleFile}`); const defaultDeveloperInstructions = buildDeveloperInstructions( { sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic" }, { includeOpenClawTurnContextBridge: true, useSoulPromptContextForPersonality: true }, ); const codexModeDeveloperInstructions = buildDeveloperInstructions( { sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic" }, { includeOpenClawTurnContextBridge: true, useSoulPromptContextForPersonality: false }, ); const projection = projectContextEngineAssemblyForCodex({ assembledMessages: [], originalHistoryMessages: [], prompt: "hello from real smoke", systemPromptAddition: "context-engine system", systemPromptAdditionMode: "context", }); const lightweightStart = buildThreadStartParams( { provider: "openai", modelId: "gpt-5.4-codex", authProfileStore: { version: 1, profiles: {} }, bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight", bootstrapContextRunKind: "cron", sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic", }, { cwd: process.cwd(), dynamicTools: [], appServer: { approvalPolicy: "on-request", approvalsReviewer: "user", sandbox: "workspace-write", }, developerInstructions: "lightweight proof", }, ); console.log(JSON.stringify({ openclawCheckout: process.cwd(), commit: "ec96840b1b", distChunks: { configFile, lifecycleFile }, defaultSoulPersonality: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({}), hasSoulPromptContext: true, }), explicitNonePersonality: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({ personalityMode: "none" }), hasSoulPromptContext: false, }), codexModePersonality: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({ personalityMode: "codex" }), hasSoulPromptContext: true, }) ?? null, defaultBridgeMentionsHostRuntime: defaultDeveloperInstructions.includes("OpenClaw is the host runtime"), defaultBridgePointsToSoul: defaultDeveloperInstructions.includes("use SOUL.md from the OpenClaw turn context"), codexModeBridgeMentionsHostRuntime: codexModeDeveloperInstructions.includes("OpenClaw is the host runtime"), codexModeBridgePointsToSoul: codexModeDeveloperInstructions.includes("use SOUL.md from the OpenClaw turn context"), rawSoulTextInDeveloperInstructions: defaultDeveloperInstructions.includes("Soul voice goes here."), contextEngineDeveloperAddition: projection.developerInstructionAddition ?? null, contextEnginePromptHasReferenceHeader: projection.promptText.includes("Context-engine guidance:"), contextEnginePromptHasSystemAddition: projection.promptText.includes("context-engine system"), lightweightProjectDocMaxBytes: lightweightStart.config?.project_doc_max_bytes ?? null, lightweightProjectDocSuppressesAgents: lightweightStart.config?.project_doc_max_bytes === 0, }, null, 2)); ' ``` **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the built OpenClaw checkout: ```text OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (ec96840) — All your chats, one OpenClaw. { "openclawCheckout": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation", "commit": "ec96840b1b", "distChunks": { "configFile": "config-Om76xpjW.js", "lifecycleFile": "thread-lifecycle-CYnqtUYN.js" }, "defaultSoulPersonality": "none", "explicitNonePersonality": "none", "codexModePersonality": null, "defaultBridgeMentionsHostRuntime": true, "defaultBridgePointsToSoul": true, "codexModeBridgeMentionsHostRuntime": true, "codexModeBridgePointsToSoul": false, "rawSoulTextInDeveloperInstructions": false, "contextEngineDeveloperAddition": null, "contextEnginePromptHasReferenceHeader": true, "contextEnginePromptHasSystemAddition": true, "lightweightProjectDocMaxBytes": null, "lightweightProjectDocSuppressesAgents": false } ``` **Observed result after fix:** The real built OpenClaw checkout reports the default SOUL path resolves to Codex `personality: "none"`, the `"codex"` config path leaves native personality unset, developer bridge text is present and points to SOUL only for the default SOUL override path, raw SOUL contents are absent from developer instructions, context-engine guidance is present in turn reference context while developer-instruction addition is `null`, and the lightweight Codex path does not suppress native project-doc loading by sending `project_doc_max_bytes: 0`. **What was not tested:** No live paid model request or remote Codex app-server network turn was run for this PR. The live proof exercises the built OpenClaw runtime/prompt-construction path locally; request-construction behavior is covered by focused app-server tests. ## Behavior Addressed Before: - Raw `SOUL.md` was not in developer instructions, which was good. - But Codex's named native personality overlay could still remain active above the SOUL turn context. - Context-engine `systemPromptAddition` could be appended to Codex developer instructions. - Lightweight Codex turns sent `project_doc_max_bytes: 0`, which suppressed native `AGENTS.md` loading while the OpenClaw-side bootstrap was also skipped. After: - Raw SOUL text remains turn context, not developer policy. - The developer bridge says how to interpret OpenClaw context and when to use SOUL for voice. - Default Codex personality is neutralized only when SOUL is present, while `personalityMode: "codex"` keeps the native personality overlay and omits the SOUL personality pointer. - Context-engine dynamic additions are turn reference context for native Codex. - Matching `thread_bootstrap` resumes keep current-turn context-engine guidance as reference context without reprojecting old bootstrap history. - Lightweight Codex turns preserve exact user input without disabling native Codex project docs. ## Real Environment Tested Checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation` Branch: `codex/soul-personality-bridge` Commit: `ec96840b1b [codex] route native personality through soul context` ## Exact Steps Or Commands Run After This Patch ```bash git diff --check OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts pnpm check:test-types ``` ## Evidence After Fix - Focused Vitest: `7 passed (7)` files, `295 passed (295)` tests. - Source delivery plan test is included in the focused Vitest command; local duration for that file after hardening was `279ms` in the pre-rebase run. - Type test check: `pnpm check:test-types` passed. - Whitespace check: `git diff --check` passed. ## Observed Result After Fix - Codex app-server thread start includes `personality: "none"` when SOUL is injected under the default config. - `personalityMode: "codex"` leaves native Codex personality unset even when SOUL is present. - Developer instructions contain the OpenClaw/Codex bridge and no raw SOUL contents; `personalityMode: "codex"` omits the SOUL personality pointer. - Cron/lightweight bootstrap path does not add the OpenClaw context bridge or personality override, and no longer disables native Codex project docs. - Context-engine `systemPromptAddition` is absent from Codex developer instructions and present in turn reference context, including matching `thread_bootstrap` resume turns. ## What Was Not Tested - No full local repo-wide suite was run; this was intentionally kept to focused local validation plus type checks under the Lexar resource policy. - No live Codex app-server manual smoke was run in this PR turn; the change is covered by the app-server request-construction and run-attempt tests.
target.author: 100yenadmin

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed
